mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets that boost consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive equity returns within one week as earnings revisions accelerate.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE drops > 0.2pp over a 30-day window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls confirm broad-based economic expansion, validating higher corporate profit forecasts and lifting equity valuations over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns across three consecutive rolling 5-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth indicates robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which historically precedes positive equity returns as supply-side efficiency expands profit margins.
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when INDPRO trends positively for two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth directly increases aggregate corporate revenues, which translates into higher earnings per share and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon without regime dependence.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward SPX returns show no positive correlation with sequential GDPC1 growth rates over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in consumer prices triggers expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns are positive in > 55% of months where CPIAUCSL increases > 0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in employment and consumer credit balances confirms expanding household purchasing power, which fuels retail and service sector earnings and lifts equity prices over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and RSAFS growth signal yields a negative information coefficient for 20-day forward SPX returns in two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising housing starts and flattening industrial production signals a speculative real estate bubble decoupling from broad economic fundamentals, triggering a negative equity correction within one week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative when HOUST growth exceeds the 75th percentile while INDPRO growth falls below the 25th percentile.
